children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program in the Office of Indian Energy and Economic Development under which the Secretary shall provide financial assistance in the form of competitive grants to eligible applicants for the establishment and operation of business incubators that serve reservation communities by providing business incubation and other business services to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs.

children.label: i
children.text: an Indian tribe;

children.label: ii
children.text: a tribal college or university;

children.label: iii
children.text: an institution of higher education; or

children.label: I
children.text: provides business and financial technical assistance; and

children.label: II
children.text: will commit to serving 1 or more reservation communities;

children.label: B
children.text: be able to provide the physical workspace, equipment, and connectivity necessary for Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs to collaborate and conduct business on a local, regional, national, and international level; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of an entity described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of subparagraph (A), have been operational for not less than 1 year before receiving a grant under the program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Two or more entities may submit a joint application for a project that combines the resources and expertise of those entities at a physical location dedicated to assisting Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs under the program.

children.label: i
children.text: contain a certification that each participant of the joint project is one of the eligible entities described in paragraph (1)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrate that together the participants meet the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: is an eligible applicant;

children.label: ii
children.text: will designate an executive director or program manager, if such director or manager has not been designated, to manage the business incubator; and

children.label: I
children.text: to a site evaluation by the Secretary as part of the final selection process;

children.label: II
children.text: to an annual programmatic and financial examination for the duration of the grant; and

children.label: III
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, to remedy any problems identified pursuant to the site evaluation under subclause (I) or an examination under subclause (II);

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the 1 or more reservation communities to be served by the business incubator;

children.label: i
children.text: the number of Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs to be participating in the business incubator;

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the business incubator will focus on a particular type of business or industry;

children.label: iii
children.text: a detailed breakdown of the services to be offered to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs participating in the business incubator; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a detailed breakdown of the services, if any, to be offered to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs not participating in the business incubator;

children.label: i
children.text: conducting financial, management, and marketing assistance programs designed to educate or improve the business skills of current or prospective businesses;

children.label: ii
children.text: working in and providing services to Native American communities;

children.label: iii
children.text: providing assistance to entities conducting business in reservation communities;

children.label: iv
children.text: providing technical assistance under Federal business and entrepreneurial development programs for which Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs are eligible; and

children.label: v
children.text: managing finances and staff effectively; and

children.label: E
children.text: a site description of the location at which the eligible applicant will provide physical workspace, including a description of the technologies, equipment, and other resources that will be available to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs participating in the business incubator.

children.label: I
children.text: to operate a business incubator that effectively imparts entrepreneurship and business skills to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs, as demonstrated by the experience and qualifications of the eligible applicant;

children.label: II
children.text: to commence providing services within a minimum period of time, to be determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: III
children.text: to provide quality incubation services to a significant number of Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs;

children.label: ii
children.text: the experience of the eligible applicant in providing services in Native American communities, including in the 1 or more reservation communities described in the application; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the proposed location of the business incubator.

children.label: I
children.text: consider the program goal of achieving broad geographic distribution of business incubators; and

children.label: II
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), give priority to eligible applicants that will provide business incubation services on or near the reservation of the 1 or more communities that were described in the application.

children.label: I
children.text: the location of the business incubator will not prevent the eligible applicant from providing quality business incubation services to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs from the 1 or more reservation communities to be served; and

children.label: II
children.text: siting the business incubator in the identified location will serve the interests of the 1 or more reservation communities to be served.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before making a grant to an eligible applicant, the Secretary shall conduct a site visit, evaluate a video submission, or evaluate a written site proposal (if the applicant is not yet in possession of the site) of the proposed site to ensure the proposed site will permit the eligible applicant to meet the requirements of the program.

children.label: i
children.text: sufficient detail for the Secretary to ensure in the absence of a site visit or video submission that the proposed site will permit the eligible applicant to meet the requirements of the program; and

children.label: I
children.text: in possession of the proposed site; and

children.label: II
children.text: operating the business incubator at the proposed site.

children.heading: Followup
children.label: C Followup
children.text: Not later than 1 year after awarding a grant to an eligible applicant that submits an application with a written site proposal, the Secretary shall conduct a site visit or evaluate a video submission of the site to ensure the site is consistent with the written site proposal.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 1 Duration
children.text: Each grant awarded under the program shall be for a term of 3 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall disburse grant funds awarded to an eligible applicant in annual installments.

children.heading: More frequent disbursements
children.label: B More frequent disbursements
children.text: On request by the applicant, the Secretary may make disbursements of grant funds more frequently than annually, on the condition that disbursements shall be made not more frequently than quarterly.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an eligible applicant that receives a grant under the program shall provide non-Federal contributions in an amount equal to not less than 25 percent of the grant amount disbursed each year.

children.label: i
children.text: the proposed business incubator will provide quality business incubation services; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the 1 or more reservation communities to be served are unlikely to receive similar services because of remoteness or other reasons that inhibit the provision of business and entrepreneurial development services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may renew a grant award under the program for a term not to exceed 3 years.

children.label: i
children.text: the results of the annual evaluations of the eligible applicant under subsection (f)(1);

children.label: ii
children.text: the performance of the business incubator of the eligible applicant, as compared to the performance of other business incubators receiving assistance under the program;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether the eligible applicant continues to be eligible for the program; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the evaluation considerations for initial awards under subsection (c)(2).

children.heading: Non-Federal contributions for renewals
children.label: C Non-Federal contributions for renewals
children.text: An eligible applicant that receives a grant renewal under subparagraph (A) shall provide non-Federal contributions in an amount equal to not less than 33 percent of the total amount of the grant.

children.heading: No duplicative grants
children.label: 5 No duplicative grants
children.text: An eligible applicant shall not be awarded a grant under the program that is duplicative of existing Federal funding from another source.

children.label: A
children.text: to provide physical workspace and facilities for Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs participating in the business incubator;

children.label: B
children.text: to establish partnerships with other institutions and entities to provide comprehensive business incubation services to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs participating in the business incubator; and

children.label: C
children.text: for any other uses typically associated with business incubators that the Secretary determines to be appropriate and consistent with the purposes of the program.

children.label: A
children.text: offer culturally tailored incubation services to Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs;

children.label: B
children.text: use a competitive process for selecting Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs to participate in the business incubator;

children.label: C
children.text: provide physical workspace that permits Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs to conduct business and collaborate with other Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs;

children.label: I
children.text: applying for and securing business credit and investment capital;

children.label: II
children.text: preparing and presenting financial statements; and

children.label: III
children.text: managing cash flow and other financial operations of a business;

children.label: ii
children.text: management education, including training and counseling in planning, organization, staffing, directing, and controlling each major activity or function of a business or startup; and

children.label: I
children.text: identifying and segmenting domestic and international market opportunities;

children.label: II
children.text: preparing and executing marketing plans;

children.label: III
children.text: locating contract opportunities;

children.label: IV
children.text: negotiating contracts; and

children.label: V
children.text: using varying public relations and advertising techniques;

children.label: E
children.text: provide direct mentorship or assistance finding mentors in the industry in which the Native business or Native entrepreneur operates or intends to operate; and

children.label: F
children.text: provide access to networks of potential investors, professionals in the same or similar fields, and other business owners with similar businesses.

children.heading: Technology
children.label: 3 Technology
children.text: Each eligible applicant shall leverage technology to the maximum extent practicable to provide Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs with access to the connectivity tools needed to compete and thrive in 21st-century markets.

children.label: A
children.text: describe the performance of the eligible applicant; and

children.label: B
children.text: be used in determining the ongoing eligibility of the eligible applicant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date on which the Secretary awards a grant to an eligible applicant under the program, and annually thereafter for the duration of the grant, each eligible applicant receiving an award under the program shall submit to the Secretary a report describing the services the eligible applicant provided under the program during the preceding year.

children.label: I
children.text: the number of Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs participating in or receiving services from the business incubator and the types of services provided to those Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs;

children.label: II
children.text: the number of Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs established and jobs created or maintained; and

children.label: III
children.text: the performance of Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs while participating in the business incubator and after graduation or departure from the business incubator; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other information the Secretary may require to evaluate the performance of a business incubator to ensure appropriate implementation of the program.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: C Limitations
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall not require an eligible applicant to report under subparagraph (A) information provided to the Secretary by the eligible applicant under other programs.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: D Coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate with the heads of other Federal agencies to ensure that, to the maximum extent practicable, the report content and form under subparagraphs (A) and (B) are consistent with other reporting requirements for Federal programs that provide business and entrepreneurial assistance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which the Secretary first awards funding under the program, and biennially thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives a report on the performance and effectiveness of the program.

children.label: i
children.text: account for each program year; and

children.label: I
children.text: the number of Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs that received business incubation or other services;

children.label: II
children.text: the number of businesses established with the assistance of the business incubator;

children.label: III
children.text: the number of jobs established or maintained by Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs receiving business incubation services, including a description of where the jobs are located with respect to reservation communities;

children.label: IV
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, the amount of capital investment and loan financing accessed by Native businesses and Native entrepreneurs receiving business incubation services; and

children.label: V
children.text: an evaluation of the overall performance of the business incubator.
